title: Reflection Accounting Inflation: Charity Tokens Minted Without _rTotal Backing Causes Systematic Holder Dilution
claim: The _takeCharity() function adds reflection tokens (_rOwned) to the contract without increasing _rTotal, while _reflectFee() decreases _rTotal by only the 1% reflection fee. For each transfer with _teamDev=8 and _taxFee=1, the sender loses rAmount = tAmount*rate, the recipient gains rTransferAmount = 0.99*rAmount, and the contract gains rCharity = 0.08*rAmount via _takeCharity. But _rTotal only decreases by rFee = 0.01*rAmount. This means sum(_rOwned) across all addresses increases by 0.07*rAmount per transfer while _rTotal only decreases by 0.01*rAmount — a net inflation of 0.08*rAmount per transfer. The contract accumulates 'unbacked' tokens that are fully functional (balanceOf reflects them, they can be transferred/sold) but have no backing in _rTotal. When the auto-swap trigger (5 tokens threshold) fires, these unbacked tokens are sold for real ETH via swapTokensForEth(), extracting value from the Uniswap pool and diluting all genuine holders.
impact: Every non-exempt transfer permanently inflates the reflection token supply by 7% of the transfer amount. The contract accumulates unbacked tokens that are periodically dumped into the Uniswap pool via auto-swap, converting them to real ETH at the expense of LPs and holders. Over time this causes continuous value leakage. Verified on-chain: _taxFee=1 (slot 19), _teamDev=8 (slot 20), auto-swap threshold=5e18 raw (slot 25).
paths: Any transfer between non-fee-exempt addresses triggers: _transfer() → _tokenTransfer() → _transferStandard() → _takeCharity(tCharity) adds rCharity to _rOwned[address(this)] without increasing _rTotal, while _reflectFee(rFee, tFee) decreases _rTotal by only the 1% reflection portion, creating 7% net inflation per transfer.

Contract auto-swap path: accumulated unbacked balanceOf(address(this)) reaches 5-token threshold → _transfer() auto-swap fires → swapTokensForEth() sells ALL contract tokens (including unbacked ones) for ETH → sendETHToTeamDev() sends half ETH to team wallet → remaining ETH permanently locked in contract. Each auto-swap extracts value from the pool equivalent to the inflated token supply.

FlawVerifier documents this via PATH_BUY + PATH_SELL and PATH_WALLET + PATH_SELL exploit chains that attempt to profit from the accounting discrepancy through flash-loan buy-sell arbitrage (src/FlawVerifier.sol:68-156). Currently infeasible at scale due to 9% combined fees making the round-trip negative-sum, but the underlying inflation mechanism is active on every transfer.

title: Uniswap Pair Reserve Divergence via Reflection Rate Changes Enables skim() Token Extraction
claim: Shoco's balanceOf() for non-excluded accounts computes tokenFromReflection(_rOwned[account]) = _rOwned[account] / _getRate(), where _getRate() = rSupply / tSupply. Each transfer burns 1% of _rTotal via _reflectFee(), changing the rate and thus balanceOf() for ALL non-excluded addresses including the Uniswap pair. However, the pair's stored reserves (getReserves()) only update during swap/mint/burn/sync operations. Any transfer that changes _rTotal without going through the pair — wallet-to-wallet transfers when uniswapOnly=false, or even trades where the pair is on only one side — causes TOKEN.balanceOf(pair) to diverge from the pair's stored reserves. An attacker can call pair.skim() to extract this excess (balance - reserve) directly from LP reserves. The FlawVerifier's _harvestExistingPairExcess() function explicitly tests this attack vector.
impact: LP reserves can be systematically drained through repeated transfer-induced divergence followed by skim() calls. On the current fork, uniswapOnly=true blocks wallet-to-wallet transfers (verified on-chain: slot with uniswapOnly=1), limiting divergence accumulation to normal trading activity. However, the vulnerability exists in the contract logic and would activate immediately if uniswapOnly were set to false or through natural trading-induced rate changes.
paths: Wallet-transfer path (if uniswapOnly=false): Attacker buys SHOCO → transfers between own wallets repeatedly (each transfer burns 1% _rTotal, changing rate, increasing balanceOf(pair)) → calls pair.skim(attacker) to extract excess tokens → sells extracted tokens back to pair for profit.
Trading path: Normal buy/sell activity causes _rTotal changes → pair's balanceOf diverges from reserves over time → attacker monitors and calls skim() when profitable.
FlawVerifier verification: _harvestExistingPairExcess() at src/FlawVerifier.sol:288-295 checks if livePairBalance > reserveToken, and if so, calls skim() to harvest the excess.

title: Permanently Locked ETH in Contract Due to Half-Split sendETHToTeamDev with No Withdrawal Mechanism
claim: The auto-swap mechanism in _transfer() calls swapTokensForEth() then sendETHToTeamDev(address(this).balance). sendETHToTeamDev() transfers only amount.div(2) (half) to _teamDevAddress, leaving the other half in the contract. The manualSend() function also only sends half. There is no function to withdraw the remaining ETH balance. Since ownership is renounced (on-chain owner=address(0), verified via storage slot 0), no one can call _setExtWallet() to redirect funds or add a withdrawal mechanism. Each auto-swap permanently locks 50% of the swapped ETH.
impact: ETH accumulates irreversibly in the contract. While not directly exploitable for profit, this represents a permanent loss of value that could otherwise benefit token holders or LPs. On a live token with significant volume, this could lock substantial ETH over time. Currently the pool has negligible ETH (~0.0004 WETH) so the impact is minimal, but the code pattern is fundamentally broken.
paths: Any transfer that pushes contract token balance over 5-token threshold → auto-swap fires → swapTokensForEth() sells all contract tokens for ETH → sendETHToTeamDev(address(this).balance) sends half to team → remaining half locked in contract forever.
owner calls manualSend() [currently impossible: owner=0x0] → same half-split behavior, half locked.
